description: Controlled severity values for policy decisions. The source marks the final severity taxonomy as open; this pack includes open to remain compatible with the current schema baseline and examples.

values.definition: Audit-relevant decision without blocking, escalation or review requirement.
values.use_when: Use when the decision is recorded for traceability only.

values.definition: Non-blocking control finding with no immediate human review requirement.
values.use_when: Use when execution may continue and the finding remains auditable.

values.definition: Control finding that requires explicit rationale and may require review depending on triggered rules.
values.use_when: Use when risk is material but no mandatory HITL trigger is active.

values.definition: Control finding that requires human review, escalation or release-gate attention.
values.use_when: Use when a HITL trigger, policy issue or missing required evidence prevents direct allow.

values.definition: Blocking control finding or rollback candidate.
values.use_when: Use when the decision is block, rollback_candidate or unresolved high-risk release control.

values.definition: Severity is not yet classified or the applicable taxonomy has not been finalized.
values.use_when: Use when the current schema baseline must remain compatible with open policy-decision severity values.

schema_application.rule: Do not hard-apply this enum to the baseline schema unless severity: open remains valid.

source_note.text: The approved control contract states that the final severity taxonomy is open.
